channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: US will send survivors of strike on suspected drug vessel back to Ecuador and Colombia, Trump says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The two survivors of an American military strike on a suspected drug-carrying vessel in the Caribbean will be sent to Ecuador and Colombia, their home countries, President Donald Trump said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: When could the shutdown end? Five key dates to watch
item.description: The shutdown became one of the three longest in U.S. history on Friday, with lawmakers indicating they believe it will drag on and few signs emerging of progress toward reopening the government.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, PIX 11

item.title: Shutdown impact: What it means for workers, federal programs and the economy
item.description: The federal government shutdown is approaching the second longest on record and there's no end in sight.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sherrill pulls out all stops with Obama endorsement, star-studded New Jersey campaign push as race tightens
item.description: Rep. Mikie Sherrill, D-N.J., campaigns with potential 2028 presidential candidates Govs. Gretchen Whitmer and Wes Moore in final push in New Jersey gubernatorial race.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In AP interview, Harris says Democrats 'are standing up for working people' in government shutdown
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — As Democrats dig in for a lengthening government shutdown, former Vice President Kamala Harris is cheering them on as she travels the country touting her presidential campaign memoir amid speculation about another White House run.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First Republican enters race for governor of New Mexico in 2026 as Democrat terms out of office
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — New Mexico has its first Republican contender for governor ahead of the 2026 elections, as the three-term city mayor of fast-growing Rio Rancho launched his campaign.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Donald Trump commutes former New York GOP Rep. George Santos' prison sentence
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Friday he had signed the commutation of disgraced former Rep. George Santos after he was sentenced to 87 months in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he has commuted sentence of former US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday he had commuted the sentence of former U.S. Rep. George Santos, who is serving more than seven years in federal prison after pleading guilty to fraud and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bolton pleads not guilty in Justice Department case accusing him of sharing government secrets
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — John Bolton pleaded not guilty Friday to charges accusing the former Trump national security adviser turned critic of emailing classified information to family members and keeping top secret documents at his Maryland home.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Bolton pleads not guilty
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeSantis calls for tax relief; House unveils 8 proposals
item.description: Florida's House Speaker Danny Perez dropped eight new property tax proposals for the legislature to consider next session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dem judge in hot seat after DHS exposes 'whole new level' of activism, sheltering illegal immigrant
item.description: The Trump DHS is criticizing a Democratic judge for taking "activism to a whole new level" by harboring an illegal gang member pled guilty to weapons charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow National Guard in Chicago
item.description: Trump is requesting the Supreme Court toss out an order by a lower court judge that prevented the Guard from coming to the city for at least two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court sets date to hear FTC Slaughter case in test of Trump's firing powers
item.description: The Supreme Court takes up Trump’s effort to remove an FTC member, a case that could expand presidential control over independent agencies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US farmers still waiting on trade war aid as USDA blames shutdown
item.description: As the U.S.-China trade war continues to squeeze American farmers, many say they are running out of time and money while waiting for relief promised by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Russia proposes 'Putin-Trump Tunnel' with Elon Musk's help to connect nations under Bering Strait
item.description: President Vladimir Putin's investment envoy outlined a plan for U.S.-Russia tunnel connecting Alaska and Siberia through Bering Strait within eight years at $8 billion cost.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A war on drugs or a war on terror? Trump's military pressure on Venezuela blurs the lines
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Under President Donald Trump, the drug war is looking a lot like the war on terror. To support strikes against Latin American gangs and drug cartels, the Trump administration is relying on a legal argument that gained traction after the Sept. 11, 2001, attacks, which allowed U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, ERIC TUCKER and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats say Trump needs to be involved in shutdown talks. He's shown little interest in doing so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is showing little urgency to broker a compromise that would end the government shutdown, even as Democrats insist no breakthrough is possible without his direct involvement. Three weeks in, Congress is at a standstill.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP senators worry about Trump, Hegseth shutdown moves
item.description: Republican senators have questions and concerns about how President Donald Trump is managing to pay more than 1 million military service members during the government shutdown and are seeking more information about what funds he is dipping into to achieve his political ends.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Truck tariff could hit an already struggling industry hard
item.description: The tariffs will raise the cost of trucks and repairs, which could be passed on to the consumer in the form of higher prices.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Thune to force Democrats to vote on paying the troops during the shutdown
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) plans to force Democrats to vote next week on legislation to pay more than 1 million military service members during the government shutdown, doubling down on his strategy of highlighting the plight of military families, according to Senate GOP sources.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senators will force a vote to prevent war on Venezuela
item.description: Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia says new legislation is needed to prevent the Trump administration’s escalating actions against Venezuela from leading to war.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: New York AG Letitia James denies federal charges, vows to defend herself
item.description: James called it a baseless case of revenge for her successful litigation against President Donald Trump and his companies.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Trump expresses optimism for Ukraine deal as Zelenskyy arrives in DC
item.description: “We need Tomahawks for the United States of America too,” Trump said. “We have a lot of them, but we need them. I mean, we can’t deplete our country.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: San Diego International Airport among airports not displaying Kristi Noem video
item.description: San Diego International Airport (SAN) has joined other airports in not displaying a video of Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Kristi Noem blaming Democrats for the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Speaker Johnson: ‘We roundly condemn’ Young Republicans group chat, swastika American flag
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Thursday that Republicans “roundly condemn” the group chat among Young Republican officials that contained racist, antisemitic and homophobic comments, as well as an American flag with a swastika that was pictured behind an aide in a GOP congressman’s office.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: ICE announces arrests as data shows most detainees lack criminal records
item.description: New data from the Transactional Records Access Clearinghouse at Syracuse University suggests a shift in ICE's enforcement priorities.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to meet Putin in Hungary in effort to end 'inglorious' war
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he intends to meet with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Hungary in the coming weeks to discuss efforts to end the war in Ukraine. Trump made the announcement following a lengthy phone call with Putin.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Sen. McConnell falls walking to Capitol for votes
item.description: (NewsNation) — Sen. Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., fell as he walked to the Capitol for votes on Thursday. It happened in the basement of the Russell Senate office building. The video shows McConnell holding an aide's hand as he walks.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: The shutdown is good for business for flood insurance
item.description: With the government shutdown, the National Flood Insurance Program is no longer writing new policies. It's causing problems for would-be homeowners, but private companies have stepped in to help.
item.dc:creator: Greg Allen, MPR News

item.title: Shutdown could last through Thanksgiving, Luna says
item.description: The federal government’s shutdown has entered its third week. Rep. Anna Paulina Luna is back in her Pinellas County district until Speaker Mike Johnson calls members back to Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: News organizations, including Hegseth’s former employer Fox, reject new Pentagon reporting rules
item.description: Fox News, the former employer of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, has joined a near-unanimous outpouring of news organizations rejecting new rules for journalists based in the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: California to regulate AI chatbots after bill by San Diego legislator
item.description: Legislation authored by State Senator Steve Padilla, who represents the San Diego area, regulating Artificial Intelligence (AI) chatbot interactions was signed into law by Governor Gavin Newsom on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas airport won't play TSA video blaming Democrats for shutdown
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- The Las Vegas airport will not play a video where Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem blames Democrats for the federal government shutdown. Leaders at Harry Reid International Airport followed their counterparts at airports in Chicago and Phoenix in making their decision.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Grand jury proceedings and subpoenas surround Hope Florida
item.description: Hope Florida, a program aiming to help folks get off government assistance, has faced scrutiny this past year over a mysterious money trail that started with a Medicaid settlement and ended with a political action committee.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illinois lawmakers to tackle immigration and budget deficit in veto session
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Illinois General Assembly’s veto session gavels in Tuesday with a massive to-do list and significant questions about whether anything can pass.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9
